folders.description:
# Lib

The main directory containing all Dart code for the application.

# Core

Contains common utilities, constants, error handling, and global dependencies.

# Error handling and exceptions

Defines custom exceptions and failure models.

# Network and API related code

Contains network utilities like Dio interceptors and API handling.

# App theming and styling

Includes colors, typography, and global styling configurations.

# Utility functions and helpers

Helper methods for string formatting, date parsing, etc.

# Abstract use cases

Defines use case contracts that interact with repositories.

# Data Layer

Handles external data sources like APIs, databases, and local storage.

# Data Models

DTOs (Data Transfer Objects) that map API responses to domain entities.

# Repository Implementations

Concrete implementations of domain repositories, handling data fetching logic.

# Data Sources

Responsible for fetching data from remote APIs and local storage.

# Remote Data Sources

Handles API calls and responses.

# Local Data Sources

Handles database and shared preferences.

# Domain Layer

Contains pure business logic, independent of any external dependencies.

# Entities

Defines core business objects without any dependencies.

# Repository Interfaces

Defines contracts that data repositories must implement.

# Use Cases

Contains business logic operations such as FetchUsersUseCase.

# Presentation Layer

Handles UI and state management.

# Screens

Contains app screens organized by feature.

# Reusable UI Components

Shared UI components like buttons and text fields.

# Bloc State Management

Handles application state using BLoC (or other state management solutions).

# Assets

Contains static assets like images, fonts, and other resources used in the application.

# Image assets

Stores all image resources.

# Font assets

Stores custom font files.

# Icon assets

Stores application icons.
